articles.title: Lakers Take 2‑0 Lead, Expanding Their Share of the NBA Title Odds
articles.original_title: Lakers earn bigger slice of NBA Title Pie with 2-0 lead, how big are Knicks and Spurs' slices? 👀 First Things First - Fox News
articles.summary: After winning the first two games of the first‑round series, the Los Angeles Lakers have increased their probability of winning the NBA championship, according to Fox News' analysis. The article compares the Lakers' odds to those of the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs, showing a widening gap in favor of Los Angeles.
key_points: Lakers lead the series 2‑0, boosting their championship odds.
Analysts note the Lakers' odds now exceed those of the Knicks and Spurs by a significant margin.
The piece emphasizes how early series leads can shift betting markets and public perception.

Recorded career highs in points, rebounds, and assists.

articles.title: JJ Redick Expresses Disappointment Over Luka Dončić Missing MVP Finalist Spot
articles.original_title: JJ Redick was disappointed that Luka Dončić was not an MVP finalist - Silver Screen and Roll
articles.summary: Former NBA player and analyst JJ Redick voiced his surprise that Dallas star Luka Dončić was not named an MVP finalist. Redick highlighted Dončić’s statistical achievements and impact on his team as reasons he deserved consideration.
key_points: Redick cited Dončić’s scoring, playmaking, and efficiency as MVP‑caliber.
The omission sparked discussion among analysts about voting criteria.
Redick’s comments reflect broader debate over the MVP selection process.

articles.title: Michael Jordan vs. LeBron James: GOAT Comparison From Age 19 to 41
articles.original_title: Michael Jordan vs. LeBron James: Pitting the GOATs from age 19 to age 41 - HoopsHype
articles.summary: HoopsHype published a side‑by‑side statistical comparison of Michael Jordan and LeBron James covering their careers from ages 19 through 41. The piece examines scoring, efficiency, and accolades to assess each player’s greatness across comparable age spans.
key_points: Jordan’s peak scoring averages outpace LeBron’s early‑career numbers, while LeBron shows greater longevity and all‑around stats.
Both players amassed multiple championships and MVP awards within the examined age range.
The article emphasizes that differing eras and playing styles complicate a definitive GOAT verdict.
